label: System User Capabilities
info:
Demonstrates the SRS System User classes (SRS 2.4, Table 5) as separate machine clients against this deployment's Hydra.

Each probe authenticates with ITS OWN client_id/client_secret (client_credentials) and calls one endpoint of its class. One client per capability, never a shared machine identity: a flow reaches only what its class may reach, and the audit trail attributes actions to the capability rather than to 'ORCE'.

- Contract Creator (ORCE_CREATOR_CLIENT_ID): lists the approved templates it would instantiate from.
- Contract Manager (ORCE_MANAGER_CLIENT_ID): lists the contracts it manages.
- Contract Signer (ORCE_SIGNER_CLIENT_ID): attempts to start a signing ceremony and MUST be refused. eIDAS Art. 3(9) makes a signatory a natural person and Art. 26 requires their sole control, so an unattended client cannot produce an advanced signature; a legal person's instrument is a seal (Art. 3(25)), which DCS does not implement. See ADR-17. A 403 here is the expected, correct outcome - the probe reports SUCCESS on refusal and raises an error if signing ever becomes possible.

The results go to debug nodes: this tab exists to show what each class can and cannot do, and it is the BDD suite (features/12_system_based_contract_management/system_user_classes.feature) that asserts the same boundaries.

name: One probe per class
Build client_credentials request
Call the class's endpoint
Judge against the expectation
